sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, presented with neck pain, which began after a car crash in February of this year.
He reports experiencing multiple car crashes, with the most recent one occurring when he was coming home from a pain clinic due to neck and back pain.
He describes his pain as sharp, incapacitating, and rates it as a 12 out of 10.
He has been experiencing numbness in his left arm and right leg, and has had spasms in his body.
He also reports headaches, dizziness, and fatigue since the accident.
He has been taking fentanyl, but has not received a prescription for several weeks.
Past medical history: The patient has had previous car crashes, including one that required surgery, and has been in therapy for a few months.
He also mentions seeing a pain clinic and a therapist.
Review of systems: The patient reports experiencing numbness, spasms, headaches, dizziness, and fatigue.
Current medications: Fentanyl (no dose or frequency mentioned), Robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has pain on palpation of the neck, both on the bony process and the muscle.
He is unable to move his neck from side to side, and has limited range of motion when bending forward and backward.
He has no visible bruising, swelling, or laceration on the neck.
Test Results: None mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain.
Problem list: Neck sprain; active; confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours for pain management.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy and an MRI to further evaluate the condition.
I will also consider referring the patient to pain medication and local injections if the current treatment does not provide sufficient relief.
I am giving the patient time off from work until the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the MRI results are available.
